untranslatable_reason: Signal requires multi-instrument comparison (cash vs futures prices and fair value calculation). The allowed indicators are single-instrument price/technical indicators. Cannot express: (1) cash market price separately from futures price, (2) fair value formula incorporating spot price + interest rate × time fraction - dividend, (3) concentrated selling detection in cash market specifically, (4) spread magnitude between two instruments. These require dual-leg instrument data and custom fair value computation not available in single-asset indicator set.
translation_notes: This is a cash-futures arbitrage signal that fundamentally requires tracking two correlated instruments (cash equity and futures contract) and computing theoretical fair value. The backtest engine's allowed indicators are designed for single-instrument technical analysis. A proper implementation would need: (a) separate cash_price and futures_price streams, (b) a fair_value indicator computed as: spot + (spot × rate × time) - dividend, (c) volume profile or order flow analysis on the cash leg. Approximation attempted using pivot (fair value proxy) and volume (selling pressure), but this loses the core arbitrage logic.
